$comment: Source of truth for reserved workspace slugs. Edit this file only. The Go side embeds this JSON directly; the TS side (packages/core/paths/reserved-slugs.ts) is regenerated from this file by `pnpm generate:reserved-slugs`. CI re-runs the generator and fails on any diff, so the two sides cannot drift. Convention for new global routes: single word (`/login`, `/inbox`) or `/{noun}/{verb}` (`/workspaces/new`). Never add hyphenated root-level word groups (`/new-workspace`, `/create-team`) â€” they collide with common user workspace names.

groups.label: Auth flow
groups.description: `onboarding` is historical, kept reserved post-removal of the route.

groups.label: Platform / marketing routes (current + likely-future)
groups.description: `multica` is reserved as the brand name to block impersonation workspaces. `www`, `new`, `home`, `homepage`, `dashboard` are confusables or likely-future global landing/entry routes; `homepage` matches the existing `/homepage` landing variant in apps/web.

groups.label: Account / billing (likely-future global routes in the avatar menu)
Workspace route segments
groups.description: Reserving each segment name prevents `/{slug}/{view}` from being visually ambiguous (e.g. a workspace named `issues` would make `/issues/abc` mean two things). `workspaces` covers the global `/workspaces/new` workspace-creation page; `teams` is reserved for future team management.

groups.label: API / integration prefixes
groups.description: `api` above already covers `/api/*`; these guard against future top-level API alias routes (e.g. `/v1`, `/graphql`) and against accidental workspace slugs that read like API identifiers.

groups.label: Backend ops / observability
groups.description: `/health`, `/readyz`, `/healthz`, and `/ws` exist on the backend host; reserving them on the workspace slug space prevents naming confusion if/when these paths are ever proxied through the web origin.

groups.label: RFC 2142 â€” privileged email mailboxes
groups.description: Allowing user workspaces with these slugs would let attackers spoof system messaging.

groups.label: Hostname / subdomain confusables
groups.description: Even on path-based routing these names attract phishing and subdomain-takeover attempts.

groups.label: Next.js / web standards
groups.description: These entries contain characters (dots, underscores) that today's slug regex `^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$` already rejects at the format-validation step â€” so `isReservedSlug` never actually matches them. They are kept as defense-in-depth so that if the slug regex is ever relaxed (e.g. to support dotted corporate slugs like `acme.io`), these system paths stay protected.
